title: Falimentul moral al coaliției de guvernare
summary: The article criticizes Romania's governing coalition for imposing brutal tax increases on citizens—particularly property and vehicle taxes—without first reforming a corrupt and bloated public administration. Author Rodica Culcer argues that the coalition lacks moral authority, as it protects 'special' categories of citizens, tolerates PSD's destabilizing behavior, and forces ordinary taxpayers to bear the cost of systemic corruption and record deficits.
talking_points: Property taxes increased far beyond the announced 70-80% due to revaluation grids and elimination of age-based deductions, with no corresponding administrative reform
The coalition unanimously agreed to raise taxes on citizens while refusing to cut spending or reform local administration, effectively making taxpayers fund a corrupt system
PSD continuously undermines the government it belongs to through successive scandals, yet coalition partners treat it with kid gloves, becoming complicit rather than reformers
The plagiarism scandal involving Justice Minister Radu Marinescu tests the coalition's moral authority, especially after reformist members already sacrificed two deputy PMs for integrity violations
Digitalization and tax collection improvements remain in early stages with no concrete timelines, while tax hikes are immediate and brutal
concepts: special privileges for magistrates and security services
author: Rodica Culcer
